_doc: Per-bucket rationale seed for the Asset Allocation tab. Hand-written by Senior Quant grounded in compute.py factor model. Replace with v10 narrative engine output once Phase 4 ships.

SOXX.name: Semiconductors
SOXX.rationale: Semis sit at the intersection of two tailwinds: real rates rolling over (lifts long-duration tech multiples) and an industrial-production recovery (lifts cyclical chip demand). Trailing 6-month return confirms the regression result rather than contradicting it. Indicator rank #1, momentum rank #1.
SOXX.kill_factors: Real rates above 2.0%, term premium above 1.5%, or HY-IG spread above 250bp.

IGV.name: Software
IGV.rationale: Software earns its multiple when real rates fall and labor markets stay tight enough to justify enterprise IT spend. We currently rate it Market Weight: long-duration tailwind is intact but earnings revisions have flattened versus Semis. Watchlist for promotion if cap-ex revisions turn positive.
IGV.kill_factors: Real rates above 2.0% or jobless claims above 280k (4-week avg).

IBB.name: Biotech
IBB.rationale: Biotech is rated Underweight on a combination of negative earnings revisions and policy overhang from drug pricing legislation. Long-duration profile means it should benefit from falling real rates, but idiosyncratic risk (FDA decisions, pipeline failures) dominates the macro signal in this bucket.
IBB.kill_factors: Earnings revisions turn positive (+0.5% threshold) or major regulatory clarity.

XLF.name: Financials
XLF.rationale: Banks are rated Underweight despite a steepening curve because of unrealized AFS losses and SLOOS C&I tightening. The curve helps net interest margins on the margin, but the back-book damage from the 2022-23 rate cycle still suppresses tangible book value growth. We need to see SLOOS reverse before promoting.
XLF.kill_factors: SLOOS turns negative (banks easing standards) and curve sustains above +50bp.

XLV.name: HealthCare
XLV.rationale: Pharma and managed care rated Underweight on negative earnings revisions and policy overhang. Defensive characteristics aren't valued in the current calm regime — when Risk & Liquidity is benign, the market pays for cyclical earnings power, not defensive yield.
XLV.kill_factors: R&L composite enters Stressed zone (defensive bid returns) or earnings revisions turn positive.

XLI.name: Industrials
XLI.rationale: Capital goods earn their Overweight on ISM new orders firming above 50, easing financial conditions, and the curve steepening — all leading indicators for capex and reshoring demand. The risk is SLOOS C&I tightening choking off the credit channel before orders convert to revenue.
XLI.kill_factors: SLOOS C&I tightening above +20pp or ISM new orders below 48.

XLE.name: Energy
XLE.rationale: Oil & gas earn their Overweight on a combination of curve steepening (pro-cyclical), supply discipline from OPEC+ and US producer capex restraint, and a copper/gold ratio that signals reflation. The bucket is also a late-cycle inflation hedge if Inflation & Rates composite re-accelerates.
XLE.kill_factors: Oil price above $100 sustained (demand destruction) or copper/gold ratio rolls over.

XLY.name: ConsDisc
XLY.rationale: Discretionary is rated Market Weight — torn between strong labor markets (favorable for consumer spending) and the rate-sensitivity of housing/auto sub-sectors. The bucket has wide internal dispersion: retail names rate Neutral, autos rate Underweight on rate sensitivity.
XLY.kill_factors: Jobless claims rising trend or real rates sustained above 2.0%.

XLP.name: ConsStaples
XLP.rationale: Staples are rated Underweight in the current calm regime — defensive yield premium isn't earned when Risk & Liquidity reads Calm. Bucket includes meaningful FX-sensitive multinationals; a strong dollar is an additional headwind.
XLP.kill_factors: R&L moves to Elevated/Stressed (defensive bid returns) or USD weakens below 100.

XLU.name: Utilities
XLU.rationale: Utilities are rated Underweight on the steepening yield curve — they trade as bond proxies and lose multiple when long-end yields rise faster than short-end. Defensive yield premium also unattractive in the current calm regime. The AI/data-center capex story provides a floor but doesn't reverse the macro headwind.
XLU.kill_factors: Curve flattens back below +25bp or R&L moves to Stressed.

XLB.name: Materials
XLB.rationale: Metals & mining earn their Overweight on the rising copper/gold ratio (pro-growth signal), industrial production recovery, and curve steepening. China demand is the swing factor — a meaningful slowdown there would compress earnings disproportionately.
XLB.kill_factors: China industrial output rolls over or copper/gold ratio breaks down.

IYR.name: RealEstate
IYR.rationale: REITs rated Underweight on real rates still elevated and active CRE stress in the office sub-sector. The bucket is also a pure bond proxy — it loses when the curve steepens. Industrial and data-center REITs are the bright spots but don't dominate the index.
IYR.kill_factors: Real rates fall below 1.0% sustained or CRE stress meaningfully resolves.

IYZ.name: CommSvcs
IYZ.rationale: Telecom & media earn their Overweight on the strongest earnings revisions in the cohort, free cash flow durability, and a defensive moat that doesn't require defensive macro to monetize. The mega-cap media component (GOOGL, META) provides AI-monetization optionality.
IYZ.kill_factors: Earnings revisions turn negative or AI monetization narrative breaks down on hyperscaler results.

MGK.name: MegaCapGrowth
MGK.rationale: Mega-cap growth rated Market Weight as a barbell hedge — the constituents are already heavily represented in the Semis (Info Tech) and Telecom & Media (Comm Svcs) overweights. Adding MGK on top would double-concentrate. Held at neutral until conviction tilts diverge from concentration risk.
MGK.kill_factors: Single-name divergence (e.g. AAPL/MSFT decoupling from semis) shifts the concentration calc.

XLC.name: Media & Entertainment
XLC.fund: SPDR Communication Services Select Sector ETF
XLC.rationale: Media & Entertainment is dominated by Meta and Alphabet, where ad spend tracks employment and discretionary income. Currently market-weight: real-rate tailwind helps the long-duration profile, but a flat earnings revision trend keeps it from overweight. AI-monetization optionality is the main upside catalyst.
XLC.kill_factors: Real rates above 2.0% or VIX above 25 (ad spend slows in stress regimes).

AAPL.name: Technology Hardware & Equipment
AAPL.rationale: Hardware is positively exposed to the same factors that drive Semis (real rates rolling over, capacity utilization recovery) but with a longer lag. AAPL anchors the basket. Currently market-weight pending capex revisions firming. AI infra build-out is the upside lever for networking/storage names (ANET, NTAP).
AAPL.kill_factors: Real rates above 2.0% or capacity utilization rolls below 76% (signals demand softening).

CARZ.name: Automobiles & Components
CARZ.fund: First Trust S&P Global Auto Index ETF — TSLA, TM, GM, F, STLA, HMC
CARZ.rationale: Autos are the most rate-sensitive consumer durable: monthly payments scale with auto-loan rates which track real rates plus credit spreads. Currently underweight on still-elevated real rates and rising delinquencies in subprime auto. EV transition adds idiosyncratic risk to TSLA, BYD.
CARZ.kill_factors: Real rates above 2.0%, jobless claims above 260k, or auto delinquencies break trend.

NKE.name: Consumer Durables & Apparel
NKE.rationale: Consumer durables and apparel sit on the consumer-discretionary frontier — sensitive to both rates (financing costs for big-ticket appliances) and breakeven inflation (input costs for apparel). Currently market-weight as China demand recovery for athletic apparel offsets weakness in housing-related durables.
NKE.kill_factors: Jobless above 260k or breakeven above 3.0% (input cost squeeze).

PEJ.name: Consumer Services
PEJ.fund: Invesco Dynamic Leisure & Entertainment ETF — MAR, HLT, BKNG, MGM, MCD
PEJ.rationale: Consumer Services (hotels, restaurants, leisure, gaming) tracks discretionary income and credit availability. Currently market-weight on tight labor market supporting wages but elevated SLOOS C&I tightening keeping a lid on small-business credit which feeds restaurant capex.
PEJ.kill_factors: Jobless above 260k or SLOOS C&I above +20pp (credit channel chokes off discretionary spend).

XRT.name: Consumer Discretionary Distribution & Retail
XRT.fund: SPDR S&P Retail ETF — equal-weight retailers (AMZN-light, AAP, BBY, ORLY, TJX, ULTA, ROST)
XRT.rationale: Discretionary retail tracks employment growth and M2 (consumer purchasing power). Currently market-weight: tight labor market is bullish but slowing M2 growth caps the upside, and high-end retail (ULTA, RH) faces wallet-share competition from services.
XRT.kill_factors: Jobless above 260k or M2 YoY drops negative (cash crunch).

WMT.name: Consumer Staples Distribution & Retail
WMT.rationale: Mass merchants and grocery are defensive staples that gain share in mid-to-late cycle as consumers trade down. Currently underweight: while jobless claims stay benign and breakeven inflation eases, the bull case for consumer staples requires either a recession trade-down (not happening) or structural margin expansion (not happening yet).
WMT.kill_factors: Jobless above 260k (recession trade-down) — would flip this to OW.

PBJ.name: Food, Beverage & Tobacco
PBJ.fund: Invesco Food & Beverage ETF — KO, PEP, MO, PM, MDLZ, KHC, KDP, GIS
PBJ.rationale: Food, beverage & tobacco is the deepest defensive equity bucket — pricing power survives most macro environments. Currently underweight: not because the businesses are weak but because cyclical groups (Semis, Energy, Industrials) score better in a normal/calm regime. Bull case re-emerges if R&L composite enters the elevated zone.
PBJ.kill_factors: R&L composite above +30 (stress regime) — would flip this to OW as a defensive rotation target.

PG.name: Household & Personal Products
PG.rationale: Household and personal products is the most rate-defensive consumer staples sub-bucket — names like PG and CL benefit from low VIX (predictable input costs) and stable breakevens. Currently underweight on relative ranking: cyclicals score better; staples wait their turn.
PG.kill_factors: VIX above 25 OR breakeven above 3.0% — both would tilt this to OW.

IYG.name: Financial Services
IYG.fund: iShares US Financial Services ETF — V, MA, JPM, BLK, AXP, SPGI, MCO, ICE
IYG.rationale: Financial Services covers payments (V/MA/AXP), exchanges (ICE/CME), data/index providers (SPGI/MSCI), and asset managers. Earnings are tightly correlated with capital-markets activity and consumer transaction volume. Currently market-weight: financial conditions easy enough to support volume but capacity-utilization recovery is slow.
IYG.kill_factors: VIX above 25 (volume comp) or ANFCI above 0 (financial conditions tightening).

KIE.name: Insurance
KIE.fund: SPDR S&P Insurance ETF — equal-weight US insurance carriers
KIE.rationale: Insurance is rate-positive: rising real rates lift float yields. Currently market-weight as real rates have rolled over from 2.5% peak, removing the tailwind. Property-catastrophe pricing remains firm and underwriting margins are healthy, but the biggest source of earnings (investment income on float) has stopped expanding.
KIE.kill_factors: Real rates back above 2.0% — the float-yield tailwind would re-engage and flip this to OW.

IHI.name: Health Care Equipment & Services
IHI.fund: iShares US Medical Devices ETF — TMO, ABT, MDT, BSX, SYK, ISRG, EW
IHI.rationale: Medical devices and health-care services together are rate-sensitive (long-duration cash flows) and exposed to capacity-utilization in hospitals. Currently market-weight: stable demand from procedure volume normalization, but ageing rate-rollover is half-priced and AI/robotic surgery pipeline (ISRG) needs another quarter of confirmation.
IHI.kill_factors: Capacity utilization rolls below 77% (hospital demand softens) or jobless above 260k.

IYT.name: Transportation
IYT.fund: iShares US Transportation ETF — UPS, FDX, UNP, NSC, CSX, DAL, UAL, CHRW
IYT.rationale: Transportation is the bellwether for industrial activity — rails and parcels move first when factory orders pick up. Currently market-weight: copper/gold rising and capacity-utilization recovering both bode well, but airline fuel hedge book is upside-down on cheap nat-gas/crude moves.
IYT.kill_factors: Capacity utilization rolls below 77% or oil above $90 (airline cost compression).

WM.name: Commercial & Professional Services
WM.rationale: Commercial & Professional Services spans waste management (WM/RSG) — which is essentially a utility — through staffing (CTAS/RHI) and data/analytics (EFX/VRSK). Currently market-weight: the mix dilutes any single-factor edge, but earnings stability is high and the basket is a quality-tilt within Industrials.
WM.kill_factors: Jobless above 260k (staffing sub-bucket cracks first) or ANFCI above 0.

CBRE.name: Real Estate Management & Development
CBRE.rationale: Non-REIT real estate spans commercial brokerage (CBRE/JLL/NMRK) and online residential platforms (Z/RDFN/EXP/OPEN). Currently market-weight: real rates rolling over helps both segments (commercial transactions resume; residential listings flow), but the bull case requires another 50bp drop in real rates to unlock the inventory-stuck residential market.
CBRE.kill_factors: Real rates back above 2.0% would re-stall residential transactions; ANFCI above 0 would freeze commercial deal flow.

BIL.name: Cash proxy
BIL.role: T-bill cash equivalent — preserves capital, earns ~5% yield

TLT.name: Long Treasuries
TLT.role: Duration hedge — rallies hard if growth shock forces rate cuts

GLD.name: Gold
GLD.role: Inflation hedge and tail-risk insurance — uncorrelated to equities

LQD.name: IG Corporate Bonds
LQD.fund: iShares Investment Grade Corporate Bond ETF
